title: lasthenia minima
intro: Lasthenia minima, also known as the dwarf tansy or lesser tarweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils. The medicinal properties of Lasthenia minima have been found to have health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Lasthenia minima, such as infusions or tinctures, can be used topically or orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
